mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 8.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate earnings revisions upward and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 8.0, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and industrial production indicates synchronized demand and supply expansion, which validates corporate profit margins and triggers positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day forward returns in over 60% of triggered periods, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, rising core consumer prices force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of high-inflation observations, or the negative correlation between CPIAUCSL and 20-day returns disappears during periods where inflation exceeds 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in new housing starts below 450,000 units signals weakening construction demand and broader economic fragility, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal yields positive 5-day forward returns in over 60% of triggered instances, or the predictive power vanishes when housing starts recover above 500,000 units.

mechanism_narrative: Rising employment combined with stagnant real GDP growth indicates productivity deceleration and margin compression, which pressures equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-GDPC1 signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction effect reverses sign during periods of strong productivity growth.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in retail and food services sales reflects robust consumer demand, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising sales periods are not statistically distinct from flat sales periods.

mechanism_narrative: During supply chain disruptions, a sustained drop in industrial production below 92.0 indicates capacity constraints that squeeze corporate margins and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in over 55% of low-industrial-production observations, or the negative relationship between INDPRO and 60-day returns reverses during periods of strong global demand.
